items.description: Single line of text written above the main text block: 'opt or særpttud velg frgum or stoma.' in similar black ink and hand.
items.scholarly_note: Possibly a catchword, running title, or carried-over text from the preceding leaf.

items.description: Arabic numeral '13' written in a later hand.
items.scholarly_note: Modern pagination or foliation mark, not original to the manuscript.

items.region: left of main text column, recurring on multiple lines (approx. lines 4, 7, 11, 14, 17, 20, 23, 26, 29, 32)
items.description: Recurring markers of the form 'Þ k.ec.iii.', 'Þ k.ec. ii.', 'Þ k.ec. iii.', 'Þ k.ee ip.vii.' etc. aligned to the left of selected text lines.
items.scholarly_note: Scribe's stanza or section dividers, integrated into the layout of this manuscript.

items.description: Multiple irregular brown age spots and foxing marks of varying size.
items.scholarly_note: Typical parchment discoloration from age, handling, and environmental exposure.

items.region: bottom center to lower right quadrant
items.description: Large pale, whitish abraded zone with surface loss and faded text.
items.scholarly_note: Consistent with water staining combined with mechanical abrasion from use.

items.description: Severe abrasion with parchment thinning, whitening, and extensive loss of ink and surface. The final lines are heavily degraded.
items.scholarly_note: The most damaged area of the folio; several words at the foot are partially or wholly illegible.

items.region: all margins, especially bottom and left
items.description: Irregular, frayed, and torn edges with small material losses and creasing.
items.scholarly_note: Common handling and binding wear on a frequently consulted medieval manuscript.

items.description: At least one broad horizontal crease and multiple fine linear scratches and scoring in the abraded zones.
items.scholarly_note: Likely from folding, rubbing, or parchment preparation flaws.

items.region: middle to lower text block, right side
items.description: Small dark ink spots and possible stray drops near the text lines.
items.scholarly_note: Minor ink blots or pen-related marks incidental to copying.

scratches: Prominent horizontal crease running across the page in the lower third (approx. lines 18–23).
Multiple fine vertical and diagonal linear abrasions concentrated in the bottom abraded area.
Surface scoring and thin scratches visible within the large pale stain at bottom center-right.
damage_notes: Heavy mechanical abrasion and ink loss affecting the final 5–6 lines; parchment surface scraped away.
Bottom edge ragged with material loss and irregular tears; text at foot incomplete.
Parchment thinning visible as white/gray patches, especially lower right quadrant.
General edge wear and small losses along left and right margins; corners rounded and damaged.
summary: Folio 22 shows moderate-to-heavy wear typical of a well-used medieval parchment. Key features include a possible header/catchword line at the top, recurring left-aligned stanza markers ('Þ k.ec...'), scattered foxing, a large abraded stain at bottom, extensive lower-edge damage with ink loss, horizontal creasing, and a modern '13' in the upper right. No doodles or clear pen trials are visible; no major interlinear corrections or extensive marginalia beyond the markers and top line.
